input.prompt:
Explore the codebase structure to understand how docs and demos are organized for registry components. I need to understand:

1. Where are the docs and demo files located for existing components?
2. What is the pattern/structure for component documentation and demos?
3. Look at a few existing components to see how they're documented

Focus on finding:
- Documentation files (likely in a docs folder or similar)
- Demo files for components
- The registry structure

Return a comprehensive summary of the file structure and patterns used.

result.output:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase structure. Here is a comprehensive summary. --- ## Comprehensive Summary: Docs and Demo Organization for Registry Components ### 1. Top-Level Directory Structure ``` /home/himmel/personal/prsui/ content/docs/ -- MDX documentation pages (rendered via Fumadocs) registry/new-york/ -- Component source code AND demo files registry.json -- Registry manifest defining all components components/ -- Site-level UI components (docs layout, navigation, etc.) source.config.ts -- Fumadocs MDX configuration lib/docs.ts -- Docs directory constants and helpers ``` ### 2. Documentation Files **Location:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/` **Files (one MDX per component):** - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/accordion.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/index.mdx` (listing page) - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/inline-toast.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/link.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/logo-marquee.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/smooth-input.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/split-action.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/tooltip.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/typewriter.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/view-on-map.mdx` **Navigation metadata:** - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/meta.json` -- defines top-level sections: `["(root)", "components"]` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/meta.json` -- defines sidebar order of component pages ### 3. Demo Files **Location:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/` **Pattern:** Each component has TWO files -- the component itself and a `*-demo.tsx` file: - `accordion.tsx` (component) + `accordion-demo.tsx` (demo) - `typewriter.tsx` (component) + `typewriter-demo.tsx` (demo) - `inline-toast.tsx` (component) + `inline-toast-demo.tsx` (demo) - `logo-marquee.tsx` (component) + `logo-marquee-demo.tsx` (demo) - `editable-chip.tsx` (component) + `editable-chip-demo.tsx` (demo) - `link.tsx` (component) + `link-demo.tsx` (demo) - `split-action.tsx` (component) + `split-action-demo.tsx` (demo) - `tooltip.tsx` (component) + `tooltip-demo.tsx` (demo) - `view-on-map.tsx` (component) + `view-on-map-demo.tsx` (demo) - `smooth-input.tsx` (component, NO separate demo file -- demo is inlined in the MDX) ### 4. Registry Manifest **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry.json` Each component entry has this shape: ```json { "name": "accordion", "type": "registry:ui", "title": "Accordion", "description": "Animated accordion with spring physics...", "files": [ { "path": "registry/new-york/accordion.tsx", "type": "registry:ui", "target": "components/prsui/accordion.tsx" } ], "dependencies": ["motion", "lucide-react"] } ``` Key points: - `type` is always `"registry:ui"` for components (and `"registry:lib"` for utility files). - `files` maps source paths to their installation `target` paths (always `components/prsui/.tsx`). - Some components include additional library files (e.g., `lib/ease.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-hover-capable.ts`). - Demo files are NOT listed in `registry.json` -- they are only referenced from the MDX docs. ### 5. MDX Documentation Pattern Every component MDX file follows this exact structure: ```mdx --- title: description: --- import { } from "@/registry/new-york/-demo"; ## Installation (Alternative with full URL pointing to registry JSON) ## Usage (Code example showing how to import and use the component) ## Props (Table of props with Type, Default, Description columns) ``` **Key patterns observed:** 1. **Frontmatter:** Always `title` + `description`. 2. **Demo import:** Always imports from `@/registry/new-york/-demo`. 3. **Preview wrapper:** Always uses `` (a custom component at `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/component-preview.tsx` that renders a styled demo box with a code toggle). 4. **Installation section:** Always shows two install paths -- short form (`@prsui/`) and full URL form (`https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/.json`). 5. **Usage section:** Shows a code block with the recommended import path (`@/components/ui/` or `@/components/prsui/`). 6. **Props section:** Markdown table with columns: Prop | Type | Default | Description. Some components have additional sub-type tables (e.g., `AccordionItem`, `Logo`). ### 6. Demo File Pattern Every demo file in `registry/new-york/` follows this structure: ```tsx "use client"; import { } from "./"; // (optionally import types) export const = () => (
); ``` Key patterns: 1. Always starts with `"use client";` directive. 2. Imports the component via relative path (`./`). 3. Exports a named function component (e.g., `BouncyAccordionPreview`, `TypewriterPreview`, `InlineToastPreview`). 4. The export name convention is `Preview`. 5. Wraps the component in a centered flex container, typically with `min-h-96` for consistent preview height. 6. Provides realistic sample data (e.g., FAQ items for accordion, logo arrays for marquee). **Exception:** `smooth-input` has no separate demo file; it imports the component directly in the MDX and renders it inline within ``. ### 7. How It All Connects ``` registry.json (manifest) | |-- Lists each component with source path + target install path + dependencies registry/new-york/.tsx (source) |-- The actual React component code users install registry/new-york/-demo.tsx (demo) |-- Showcases the component with sample data |-- Imported by the MDX doc file content/docs/components/.mdx (documentation) |-- Frontmatter (title, description) |-- Imports demo from registry/new-york/-demo |-- Renders via component |-- Installation, Usage, Props sections content/docs/components/meta.json (navigation) |-- Controls sidebar ordering of component pages content/docs/meta.json (top-level nav) |-- Defines doc sections: (root) + components ``` ### 8. Site Infrastructure (Supporting Files) - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/source.config.ts` -- Fumadocs MDX configuration, points to `content/docs` directory. - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/lib/docs.ts` -- Exports `DOCS_DIR = "content/docs"`, plus helpers like `isComponentsFolder`. - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/component-preview.tsx` -- The `` wrapper used in all MDX files; renders a demo/code toggle. - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/components-list.tsx` -- The `` component used in the index page to auto-generate a grid of all component links. - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/app/(app)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx` -- The Next.js catch-all page that renders docs.

1: ---
2: title: Logo Marquee
3: description: An infinite scrolling logo marquee with smooth animation and hover pause.
4: ---
5: 
6: import { LogoMarqueePreview } from "@/registry/new-york/logo-marquee-demo";
7: 
8: <ComponentPreview name="logo-marquee">
9:   <LogoMarqueePreview />
10: </ComponentPreview>
11: 
12: ## Installation
13: 
14: If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
15: 
16: <CodeBlockCommand
17:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-marquee"
18:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-marquee"
19:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-marquee"
20:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-marquee"
21: />
22: 
23: Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
24: 
25: <CodeBlockCommand
26:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-marquee.json"
27:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-marquee.json"
28:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-marquee.json"
29:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-marquee.json"
30: />
31: 
32: ## Usage
33: 
34: After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
35: 
36: ```tsx
37: import { LogoMarquee, type Logo } from "@/components/prsui/logo-marquee";
38: 
39: const logos: Logo[] = [
40:   {
41:     alt: "Vercel",
42:     src: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/develop/icons/vercel.svg",
43:   },
44:   {
45:     alt: "Next.js",
46:     src: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/develop/icons/nextdotjs.svg",
47:   },
48: ];
49: 
50: export function MyComponent() {
51:   return <LogoMarquee logos={logos} />;
52: }
53: ```
54: 
55: ## Props
56: 
57: ### Logo
58: 
59: | Field    | Type     | Default  | Description            |
60: | -------- | -------- | -------- | ---------------------- |
61: | `src`    | `string` | -        | Image URL for the logo |
62: | `alt`    | `string` | -        | Alt text for the image |
63: | `width`  | `number` | `"auto"` | Optional width         |
64: | `height` | `number` | `"auto"` | Optional height        |
65: 
66: ### LogoMarquee
67: 
68: | Prop        | Type     | Default | Description                    |
69: | ----------- | -------- | ------- | ------------------------------ |
70: | `logos`     | `Logo[]` | -       | Array of logo objects          |
71: | `className` | `string` | -       | Classes applied to the wrapper |
72: 
73: ### InfiniteSlider (internal)
74: 
75: | Prop              | Type                         | Default        | Description                          |
76: | ----------------- | ---------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------ |
77: | `children`        | `React.ReactNode`            | -              | Content to scroll                    |
78: | `gap`             | `number`                     | `16`           | Gap between items in px              |
79: | `duration`        | `number`                     | `25`           | Animation duration in seconds        |
80: | `durationOnHover` | `number`                     | -              | Duration when hovered (slows down)   |
81: | `direction`       | `"horizontal" \| "vertical"` | `"horizontal"` | Scroll direction                     |
82: | `reverse`         | `boolean`                    | `false`        | Reverse scroll direction             |
83: | `className`       | `string`                     | -              | Classes applied to the outer wrapper |

(End of file - total 83 lines)
</content>

todos.content: Update registry.json to include logo-grid component

input.content:
---
title: Logo Grid
description: A responsive grid layout for displaying logos with highlighted cells and plus icon decorations.
---

import { LogoGridPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/logo-grid-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="logo-grid">
  <LogoGridPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-grid"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-grid"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-grid"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/logo-grid"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-grid.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-grid.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-grid.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/logo-grid.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { LogoGrid, type Logo } from "@/components/prsui/logo-grid";

const logos: Logo[] = [
  {
    alt: "Vercel",
    src: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/develop/icons/vercel.svg",
  },
  {
    alt: "Next.js",
    src: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/develop/icons/nextdotjs.svg",
  },
];

export function MyComponent() {
  return <LogoGrid logos={logos} />;
}
```

## Props

### Logo

| Field    | Type     | Default | Description            |
| -------- | -------- | ------- | ---------------------- |
| `src`    | `string` | -       | Image URL for the logo |
| `alt`    | `string` | -       | Alt text for the image |
| `width`  | `number` | -       | Optional width         |
| `height` | `number` | -       | Optional height        |

### LogoGrid

| Prop        | Type     | Default | Description                    |
| ----------- | -------- | ------- | ------------------------------ |
| `logos`     | `Logo[]` | -       | Array of logo objects (max 8)  |
| `className` | `string` | -       | Classes applied to the wrapper |
